exploration_log.aha_moment: The original implementation is a single-line rawFormat comparison. The fix is to detect time-sensitive format characters with a regex, check if timezones differ (via getOffset()), and normalize both to the same timezone before formatting.
exploration_log.hindsight: Verify which PHP date format characters are timezone-dependent (P, O, T, Z, e) to avoid incorrectly normalizing formats that explicitly compare timezone offsets.

alternatives: Always normalize to UTC for any format
Normalize only when both timezones differ AND format has time chars
key_decisions.decision: Normalize only when format has time components AND no timezone tokens AND offsets differ
key_decisions.reasoning: Normalizing unconditionally would break formats that explicitly include timezone tokens. Checking getOffset() inequality avoids an unnecessary copy when both dates are already in the same zone.

search_sequence.result: H, G, h, g, i, s, u, a/A are time chars that differ; P, O, T, Z, e are timezone tokens

solve_notes: In `isSameAs()`, when the format string contains time-sensitive characters (H, G, h, g, i, s, u, a) but no explicit timezone tokens (P, O, T, Z, e), and the two dates have different UTC offsets, both dates are converted to `$this`'s timezone before formatting. This means `isSameAs('H', $utcDate)` returns true when both represent the same hour in the same timezone view, fixing the false-negative for same-instant different-timezone comparisons.
task_text:
Fix `isSameAs()` format comparison not handling timezone-aware formats

The `isSameAs()` method in `Traits/Comparison.php` compares two dates
by formatting both with a given format string (e.g., `'Y-m-d'`). When
the format includes timezone-sensitive components (e.g., `'H'` for
hour), two dates in different timezones can incorrectly compare as
different even when they represent the same instant. Fix `isSameAs()`
to normalise both dates to the same timezone before formatting when
the format includes time components.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: isSameUnit
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: callers of isSameAs in the same file include isSameUnit; confirms the method is called with simple date-only formats too, reinforcing that the fix must be guarded on time-component detection

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set contains only the edited method. The thrash_preventing set adds isSameUnit because an AI agent would check what calls isSameAs to confirm the fix won't break date-only format comparisons — seeing isSameUnit's callers with formats like 'Y-m' or 'm' confirms the time-component regex guard is necessary.
